name: voice_clone_election_release
premise: Platform integrity chief Renee Foster has confirmed that a viral audio clip of a mayoral candidate privately mocking disabled veterans is almost certainly synthetic, generated from publicly available interviews using a consumer voice-cloning tool. The clip is spreading forty-eight hours before the election and could alter turnout in a race already within one point. Removing it immediately may help the candidate, who has previously accused the platform of political bias and whose supporters will likely claim censorship. Leaving it up with a warning label may allow millions of voters to hear and share a fabricated statement before professional fact-checks catch up. Journalists have asked whether the platform will disclose the detection system it used, but doing so could also help bad actors reverse-engineer future evasion.

agents.name: Renee Foster
agents.identity: I am the platform integrity chief trying to defend public discourse without becoming an unelected editor of political reality. I know that every delay in action protects accusations of bias while also giving the lie more oxygen.
agents.goal: I want to reduce electoral harm from synthetic deception while preserving public trust that the platform is not manipulating democratic outcomes for partisan ends. I need to decide whether speed or procedural caution matters more in the final hours before voting.

agents.name: Omar Dixon
agents.identity: I am the platform's general counsel and I have watched every moderation decision involving candidates turn into litigation, hearings, and accusations of corporate electioneering. A rushed takedown could become the story itself.
agents.goal: I want Renee to leave the clip up with a label unless the evidence crosses an extremely high threshold. I believe the company should avoid direct removal so close to the election unless the proof is overwhelming and defensible in public.

choices: I will remove the synthetic audio immediately to limit election manipulation, accepting accusations that the platform intervened in the race.
I will leave the audio up with warning labels and context while investigations continue, accepting the risk that a fabricated clip may influence voters before the election.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Renee Foster must decide whether to remove the suspected synthetic audio before the election. What does Renee Foster do?
